description_long: This is a roughly rectangular, flat stone slab with a prominent star-shaped fossil or imprint embedded in its upper surface. The stone is predominantly gray with some beige and off-white tones along its edges, suggesting natural sedimentary layering. The surface is uneven and slightly chipped, with visible cracks and a weathered appearance, indicating age and exposure to the elements. One side of the stone bears a handwritten catalog number, hinting at its use in a collection or as a specimen. The fossil imprint is deeply set and visually striking, providing a focal point on the otherwise plain stone. The overall form is irregular, with jagged edges and a natural, unpolished finish.
description: A flat, gray stone slab featuring a deep star-shaped fossil imprint and rough, weathered edges. The surface is marked by cracks and a catalog number, giving it a natural, aged appearance.
description_view_0: The top view highlights the star-shaped fossil imprint set into the flat, gray surface, with rough, chipped edges visible.
description_view_1: This angled view shows the fossil imprint and reveals the catalog number written on the stone's side, as well as the beige layering along the edge.
description_view_2: The side view emphasizes the stone's thickness and the rough, uneven lower edge, with the fossil imprint only partially visible.
description_view_3: The opposite side view displays the stone's irregular profile and natural stratification, with no visible fossil or markings.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground or display pedestal
attribute_pattern: Distinct star-shaped fossil imprint on the upper surface, with natural sedimentary layering and minor surface cracks.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with visible cracks and chipped edges, indicating prolonged exposure to natural elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured or paved urban environments, but could blend with naturalistic landscaping or rock gardens.
